MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT
Off Calendar Oct-16-2012 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES:
Eunice Chang of IMAI, TADLOCK, KEENEY & CORDERY, LLP (415-675-7000), appearing for intervenors / moving parties Hartford Fire Insurance Company, et al.
Jeffrey A. Kaiser of KAISER GORNICK LLP (415-857-7474), appearing for plaintiffs / opposing parties Robert Montgomery, et al.

RULING:
INTERVENORS HARTFORD FIRE INSURANCE COMPANY, WEST AMERICAN INSURANCE COMPANY, SAFECO INSURANCE COMPANY OF AMERICA, TRAVELERS INDEMNITY CO., ZURICH AMERICAN INSURANCE CO, SUCCESSOR IN INTEREST TO ZURICH INSURANCE COMPANY, US BRANCH'S (ON BEHALF OF THEIR INSURED, DEFENDANT RICH-TEX, INC.) MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT, OR IN THE ALTERNATIVE, SUMMARY ADJUDICATION

Hearing held and tentative ruling adopted: Intervenors' (on behalf of their insured, defendant Rich-Tex, Inc.) motion for summary judgment is denied and its alternate motion for summary adjudication is denied as to issues 1, 2 and 3 (negligence, strict liability, and survival) and granted as to issue 4 (punitive damages). As to summary judgment and issues 1, 2 and 3 of summary adjudication, Ms. Sultz's depositon testimony, Rich-Tex, Inc.'s responses to general order 129 interrogatories and Mr. Watkins' testimony create a triable issue whether Mr. Montgomery was exposed to asbestos-containing products or materials (joint compound) attributable to Rich-Tex. As to whether such exposure was a substantial factor in causing or contributing to Mr. Montgomery's risk of developing mesothelioma, Intervenors failed to sustain their initial burden under either prong of Aguilar. Intervenors did not satisfy their burden of demonstrating that plaintiffs do not possess and cannot reasonably obtain evidence that Mr. Montgomery's exposure to asbestos-containing Rich-Tex joint compound was a substantial factor. Intervenors' failure to demonstrate that plaintiffs submitted factually devoid responses to written contention discovery designed to elicit all the evidence plaintiffs have to support their claim that exposure attributable to Rich-Tex was a substantial factor precludes Intervenors from successfully invoking the factually devoid prong of Aguilar. Intervenors also did not satisfy their burden of negating an essential element of plaintiffs' claims against them. Mr. Cohen's declaration fails to take into account much of Ms. Sultz's deposition testimony regarding Mr. Montgomery's work with Rich-Tex joint compound and is thus insufficient to allow defendant to sustain its burden. As to adjudication issue 4 (punitive damages), Intervenors sustained their initial burden. Plaintiffs failed to present clear and convincing evidence creating a triable issue whether Rich-Tex acted with the requisite malice, fraud or oppression to warrant the imposition of punitive damages. Intervenors' objections to Ms. Sultz's deposition testimony are overruled. The court considered plaintiffs' opposition. Counsel for plaintiffs to prepare an order. Judge: Teri L. Jackson, Reporter: Carol L. Schilp of AIKEN WELCH, CSR 648 (503/TLJ)

MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES:
Lisa Sapcoe, Esq. of ARMSTRONG & ASSOCIATES, LLP (510-433-1830) appearing for defendant / moving party Elementis Chemicals Inc.
Matthew B. McLeod, Esq. of SHRADER & ASSOCIATES, LLP (713-782-0000), appearing for plaintiff / opposing party Robert Montgomery

RULING:
Defendant Elementis Chemicals Inc.'s motion for summary judgment.
No argument was made and tentative ruling adopted: Defendant Elementis Chemicals Inc.'s motion for summary judgment is denied. The deposition testimony of Mr. Hamilton, Mr. Persson and Ms. Sultz create a triable issue whether Robert Montgomery was exposed to an asbestos-containing product or material (Hamilton joint compound and/or raw Union Carbide Calidria) attributable to defendant. The bulk supplier defense is not available to a supplier of raw asbestos in asbestos exposure cases because asbestos is inherently dangerous and raw asbestos fibers do not change when they become a component of an asbestos-containing product. (Garza; Jenkins; Arena). Order signed in open court. Judge: Harold Kahn, Reporter: LaVena Ward, CSR # 7077 =(220/HEK)
